records.title: DAU to MAU ratio
records.summary: Track daily active users as a proportion of monthly active users to measure product stickiness and engagement depth.
records.metric_definition: Average daily active users divided by monthly active users for the same period.
records.methodology_note: Define active as at least one meaningful product interaction, excluding passive pageviews.

records.title: Customer acquisition cost payback period
records.summary: Measure months to recover fully loaded CAC from gross margin contribution to ensure unit economics sustainability.
records.metric_definition: Fully loaded CAC divided by monthly gross margin per customer.

records.title: Platform reliability and observability
records.summary: Maintain SLOs for latency, availability, and error rate with full distributed tracing and automated incident response.

records.title: Data lineage and governance at scale
records.summary: Enforce automated data cataloging, lineage tracking, and access controls as data volumes and team count scale.

records.title: Product-led growth instrumentation
records.summary: Instrument every user journey touchpoint to feed activation, conversion, and expansion models that drive self-serve revenue.

records.title: Infrastructure cost optimization
records.summary: Right-size compute, storage, and data pipeline costs against usage growth to protect margins as the platform scales.

records.title: Instrument product usage events for real-time activation and conversion
records.summary: Prioritize use cases that leverage real-time product event streams — signups, feature adoption, paywall hits, session depth — for in-product activation and conversion optimization rather than retrospective cohort analysis alone.

records.title: Surface infrastructure cost per query and pipeline to drive FinOps accountability
records.summary: Prefer use cases that attribute compute and storage costs to specific teams, features, and pipelines so engineering leaders can make data-driven trade-offs between speed, cost, and reliability.
